title: The Matrix Decision Making Framework
subtitle: A Structured System for Evaluating Alternatives Without Paralysis
author: Georgiy S.
themes: Decision paralysis as a systemic failure, not a personal one
Choice architecture and how options are framed shapes every decision
The matrix approach: structured multi-criteria evaluation of alternatives
Bounded rationality and satisficing under real-world constraints
Choice overload and the paradox of too many options
Decision quality through process design, not willpower
excerpt: Every difficult decision — from hiring to choosing a career change — shares a structural flaw: the options are evaluated without a clear framework. Georgiy S., a research lead specializing in choice architecture, shows how decision paralysis is not a personal weakness but a predictable outcome of poor system design. The Matrix Decision Making Framework gives readers a repeatable, matrix-based system for evaluating any set of alternatives across weighted criteria — replacing analysis paralysis with action.
